name: When is the product AB defined?
question_text: If A is an m × n matrix and B is a p × q matrix, the matrix product AB is defined when which condition holds?
general_feedback: For AB to be defined, the number of columns of A (n) must equal the number of rows of B (p). The result AB then has shape m × q.
correct_feedback: Correct — Columns of A must match rows of B for the dot products to line up.

answers.feedback: Columns of A must match rows of B for the dot products to line up.
The outer dimensions (m and q) describe the shape of AB; they need not be equal.
A does not need to be square for AB to be defined.
B does not need to be square for AB to be defined.
